tldr: Of your 33 live ads, the Realtree collab body copy ('Two iconic names. One unstoppable hat...') is running verbatim across 14 separate ads — Meta's Andromeda system clusters those as effectively one creative, which means most of your ad set is competing against itself for impressions. Six of those Realtree variants have been live for 27 straight days, well past the 21-day refresh window typical brands at your scale should be hitting.

benchmark.context: Typical apparel brands refresh hero creative every ~21 days; top performers hit ~14. At Webb Western's volume — 33 live ads, fresh shipments every week — the practical window is closer to 10–14 days because the Realtree concept has saturated the cluster faster than a normal launch curve.

ads.body: Official Webb Western x Realtree® collab. Two iconic names. One unstoppable hat. • Waterproof • Sweat resistant • Easy to clean • Perfect fit guarantee
drivers: Identical body copy running across 14 of your live ads — Andromeda treats this entire group as one creative concept and throttles the cluster
Live for 27 days, which is 6 days past the typical refresh window for apparel and 13 past where high-volume accounts at your scale should be cycling
Same 'JUST DROPPED' headline appears on 11 hat ads — the freshness signal is gone, returning customers have seen it for nearly a month

Carousel format with no hook variance — every slide pulls from the same product family

Andromeda's similarity scoring puts this well past the 60% threshold that triggers cluster suppression

No format variance from sister ads (all carousel, all hats LP) so Meta can't surface a winner inside the cluster

hero_concept.hook: Tested before sunrise. Built for the long haul.
hero_concept.headline: The Drift Camo Tee
hero_concept.primary_text:
No Excuses. No Bullshit. Just a tee that earns its keep before most people are out of bed.

The Performance Tee in Drift Camo is cut from a buttery-soft performance knit — lightweight, breathable, holds its shape after the truck, the dog, the dirt, and the wash. Made for the guys who actually use their gear.

Buy 2 Get 1 Free — straight from the ranch to your front porch.
hero_concept.visual_direction: The Performance Tee in Drift Camo laid flat on weathered cedar fence wood at first light, with a worn leather work glove and a chipped enamel coffee mug framing the edges — outdoor, lived-in, no model.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Every one of your hat ads pushes the same Realtree collab paragraph and every tee ad leans on 'Buy 2 Get 1 Free' as the lead — there's no creative that opens on the lifestyle and craftsmanship story your brand voice is built around. This concept lets the product breathe in its environment without leaning on a discount or a collab name to do the work.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a weathered cedar plank surface at a working ranch, with a worn brown leather work glove resting at the upper-left corner and a chipped white enamel coffee mug at the lower-right corner, the tee laid flat and centered, with golden-hour first-light streaming across at a low angle casting long soft shadows of the fabric folds, dust motes faintly visible in the light. Rugged Americana editorial aesthetic — earthy, lived-in, masculine, grounded in the hunting / ranch / outdoor lifestyle Webb Western is built for. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL — preserve the exact Drift Camo pattern, scale, and earthy green/brown/tan colorway of the performance knit fabric exactly as shown in the reference image — strictly NO color shift toward orange, blue, pink, grey, or black; NO smoothing of the camo print into a solid; NO swapping to a different camo pattern (no Realtree, no Mossy Oak, no digital camo); preserve the buttery-soft matte performance-knit finish (not glossy, not shiny). STRUCTURE — preserve the exact construction: short-sleeve crew neckline, straight regular-fit silhouette, ribbed crew collar, set-in sleeves with finished hems, straight bottom hem, and PRESERVE every printed graphic, label, brand mark, chest logo, sleeve hit, and neck label exactly as they appear on the tee in the reference image — same placement, same colors, same scale. Do NOT add pockets, do NOT add buttons or a placket, do NOT change to V-neck or henley, do NOT change sleeve length, do NOT remove or alter the printed graphics or brand labels on the tee, do NOT relocate any graphic, do NOT recolor any printed element. Render the product's metal, color, finish, fabric, weave, stones, surface treatment, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, closures, hardware count, pockets, sleeves, neckline, printed graphics) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the metal type, do NOT shift the color temperature, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove buttons, snaps, zippers, pockets, straps, or any hardware, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify printed graphics or text on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself — text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.

next_step.urgency: Six Realtree ads cross fatigue within the week — May 18–20.
next_step.headline: Walk through the Webb Western refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Dylan, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep — work with us or don't.

method_note: We went through every live ad in Webb Western's Meta Ad Library on May 13, mapped copy duplication, format mix, days-running, and landing destinations, then scored each ad against 2025–2026 Andromeda clustering behavior — no account access, no private metrics.
